input.prompt: I need to update and align NuGet package versions across the advanced-multi-complexity/Enterprise.slnx solution. Package versions have drifted out of sync — some are defined as MSBuild properties, some are conditional for multi-targeting, and there's a shared Common.props. Can you bump everything to the latest and get them consistent?

data.content: Now I have a full picture. Let me look up the latest NuGet package versions.

arguments.command: curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/system.text.json/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; print([x for x in v if 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1])"

curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.extensions.logging/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; print([x for x in v if 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1])"

curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/opentelemetry.extensions.hosting/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; print([x for x in v if 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1])"

curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.aspnetcore.mvc.newtonsoftjson/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; print('net6:', [x for x in v if x.startswith('6.') and 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1]); print('net8:', [x for x in v if x.startswith('8.') and 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1])"

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the solution. Let me identify all packages and their versions:

1. **System.Text.Json**: Web=10.0.1, Legacy=8.0.5, Tests=10.0.1, Core=9.0.0, Api=10.0.1 → drifted
2. **Microsoft.Extensions.Logging**: Common.props=9.0.0
3. **Azure.Storage.Blobs**: Legacy=12.19.0, Api uses $(BlobsVersion)=12.24.0 → drifted
4. **Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting**: Core uses $(HostingVersion)=8.0.1


5. **OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting**: Web=1.15.0
6. **xunit**: Tests=2.9.3
7. **Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson**: Api net6.0=6.0.36, Api net8.0=8.0.11

I need to check NuGet for the latest versions of these packages to see what updates are available.

Now I'm querying the NuGet API to fetch the current latest versions for each package.

curl -s "https://api.nuget.org/v3-flatcontainer/microsoft.aspnetcore.mvc.newtonsoftjson/index.json" | python3 -c "import sys,json; v=json.load(sys.stdin)['versions']; print('net6:', [x for x in v if x.startswith('6.') and 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1]); print('net8:', [x for x in v if x.startswith('8.') and 'preview' not in x and 'rc' not in x and 'alpha' not in x and 'beta' not in x][-1])"

data.content: Got all the latest versions. Now I'll centralize version properties in `Directory.Build.props` and update all projects to use them consistently.

arguments.old_str:
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="$(BlobsVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="6.0.36" />

  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'">

arguments.new_str:

    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="$(BlobsVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>

    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="$(NewtonsoftJsonNet6Version)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="$(NewtonsoftJsonNet8Version)" />

data.reasoningText:
Now I have the latest versions:
- System.Text.Json: 10.0.7 (currently 10.0.1, 9.0.0, 8.0.5)
- Microsoft.Extensions.Logging: 10.0.7 (currently 9.0.0)
- Azure.Storage.Blobs: 12.27.0 (currently 12.24.0, 12.19.0)
- Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting: 10.0.7 (currently 8.0.1)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.3 (currently 1.15.0)


- xunit: 2.9.3 (already latest)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson: net6=6.0.36 (already latest), net8=8.0.26 (currently 8.0.11)

I'll centralize all these version properties in Directory.Build.props and reference them across the project files, keeping conditional versions for the multi-targeting scenario in the Api project.

Now I'm setting up the version properties centrally with SystemTextJsonVersion, LoggingVersion, BlobsVersion, HostingVersion, OpenTelemetryHostingVersion, XunitVersion, and separate properties for NewtonsoftJson across net6 and net8, then I'll update Common.props and all the csproj files to use these centralized references instead of hardcoded values.

arguments.old_str:
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="$(BlobsVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="6.0.36" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="8.0.11" />
arguments.new_str:
<PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" Version="$(SystemTextJsonVersion)" />
    <PackageReference Include="Azure.Storage.Blobs" Version="$(BlobsVersion)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net6.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="$(NewtonsoftJsonNet6Version)" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup Condition="'$(TargetFramework)' == 'net8.0'">
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson" Version="$(NewtonsoftJsonNet8Version)" />
